title: Agent runners component catalog
description: Optional components layered on top of the single base runner (ubuntu-claude-code). The base agent is always installed; components are selected per-agent via the AGENT_COMPONENTS env list. Profiles (profiles.json) are named presets of these slugs. The portal vendors this file; agent-runners is the single source of truth.

slug.description: Stable id persisted in agents.components and threaded into AGENT_COMPONENTS. Resolves to base/components/<slug>/.

kind.description: Install dispatch class. runtime = service/binary install via the component's own scripts; packs = knowledge-pack install (needs the SB server); toolchain = OS-level dev tool (apt/repo). MCP plugins are a separate role-driven catalog (plugins.json), not components.

description.description: One-line helper text in the wizard.

requires.description: Other component slugs that must also be selected. The wizard auto-checks these; unchecking a required-by component unchecks its dependents.

unlocks.description: Portal-level abilities this component enables. sidebutton-server unlocks dispatch + capabilities; absence ⇒ capabilities=[] and the agent is not dispatchable.

chip.description: Fleet/detail dep-chip descriptor. live=true chips get a status dot from a process signal; live=false are static 'installed' declarations.

config_files.description: Runtime config files this component consumes from a declared default path (SCRUM-1597). Each entry pins a target_path the component auto-applies from at boot and via a systemd path-unit watcher; delivery (manual SSH placement or, later, portal push) validates against this same declaration. Absence ⇒ the component needs no operator-supplied config file.

config_file.description: One config-file slot a component declares + auto-consumes from its target_path. The component's contract: delivery is validated against target_path on the agent (no arbitrary paths).

id.description: Stable id for this slot within the component (persisted on the delivery row + apply state). Unique per component.

description.description: One-line helper text: what the file is and how it is applied.

accept.description: Accepted filename suffixes (e.g. [".conf"]). A client-side hint AND a defence-in-depth check in sb-config-place.

secret.description: Payload is a secret (encrypted at rest; never returned to the client). VPN/RDP profiles carry private keys ⇒ true.

scope_default.description: Default delivery scope in the Files hub (D1). Agent-scoped rows override workspace-scoped rows for the same file.

target_path.description: Path the component consumes from on the agent. A trailing '/' (or multiple:true) is a directory that accepts several files; otherwise a single pinned file. The uniform convention is /etc/sidebutton/config/<slug>/; components may keep an existing path for back-compat.

multiple.description: target_path accepts multiple files (directory) vs a single file.
